nodes.label: Entrypoints
nodes.description: CLI and desktop app entry points

nodes.label: Bridge
nodes.description: Message passing between CLI frontend and agent backend

nodes.label: Query Engine
nodes.description: AsyncGenerator coroutine driving the agent loop

nodes.label: Services
nodes.description: Business logic: compaction, MCP, OAuth, analytics

nodes.label: Context
nodes.description: React context providers for dependency injection

nodes.label: Utilities
nodes.description: Shared utilities: git, permissions, sandbox, auth, shell

journeySteps.title: 1. User launches Claude Code
journeySteps.description: The CLI entry point parses arguments and initializes the React/Ink terminal application.

journeySteps.title: 2. CLI routes the command
journeySteps.description: The command parser matches the user's input to one of 101 registered slash commands, or falls to the default prompt mode.

journeySteps.title: 3. UI renders the prompt
journeySteps.description: React/Ink components render the terminal interface: input box, message history, status line, and tool output.

journeySteps.title: 4. Bridge carries the message
journeySteps.description: The bridge layer passes the user's prompt from the CLI frontend to the agent backend, managing session state and message format.

journeySteps.title: 5. Query Engine runs the loop
journeySteps.description: The AsyncGenerator coroutine sends the conversation to the API, receives streaming responses, and dispatches tool calls in a ReAct loop.

journeySteps.title: 6. Tools execute actions
journeySteps.description: Each tool_use block is dispatched to the matching tool implementation. Read-only tools run concurrently; write tools run exclusively.

journeySteps.title: 7. Services provide capabilities
journeySteps.description: Tools and the query engine call into services for compaction, MCP communication, OAuth, analytics, and other business logic.

journeySteps.title: 8. Utilities underpin everything
journeySteps.description: Shared code for git operations, file permissions, sandbox enforcement, shell execution, and authentication flows.
